answeredQuestions.target-users: SPL, Scoutmaster, Scouts, Assistant Scoutmaster, Parents
answeredQuestions.core-problem: Meal planning consumes excessive meeting time, produces inconsistent records, creates communication breakdowns
answeredQuestions.success-metrics: Meeting time ≤15min, zero shopping errors, zero forgotten equipment, 100% recipe availability, 80% adoption, ≥50% feedback rate
answeredQuestions.scope-in: Event mgmt, meal scheduling, recipe mgmt, scaling, shopping/equipment lists, printable recipes, shareable link, feedback, content moderation, auth/RBAC
answeredQuestions.scope-out: Multi-troop, patrol mgmt, brainstorming/voting, shopper assignment, advanced search, analytics dashboards, budget tracking, offline/PWA, native apps, external integrations
answeredQuestions.assumptions: Web browser access, 3 patrols, internet during meetings, Scoutmaster currently transcribes manually
answeredQuestions.constraints: Single developer, prototype May 2026, live Sep 2026, youth PII minimal, Scouting America compliance, content moderation, mobile-first, 8-35+ headcount
answeredQuestions.stakeholders: Scoutmaster, Asst Scoutmaster, SPL, Scouts, Parents
answeredQuestions.dependencies: Scouting America compliance, Azure Content Safety, Entra ID MSAL
answeredQuestions.data-requirements: Recipe library, event data, feedback, shopping/equipment list state
answeredQuestions.design-direction: Outdoor field guide aesthetic, forest green + campfire orange, Space Grotesk + Source Sans 3, shadcn/ui, mobile-first
answeredQuestions.performance-targets: No hard numbers — responsive during meetings, fast enough not to frustrate scouts. Default Azure SWA uptime.
answeredQuestions.cicd-pipeline: GitHub Actions: ci.yml (PR build+test), deploy.yml (push-to-main: SWA frontend + Functions API), infra.yml (manual Bicep dispatch)
answeredQuestions.monitoring: Azure Application Insights for frontend and API
answeredQuestions.rollback: Redeploy previous version from main — sufficient for MVP
answeredQuestions.test-coverage: ~70% API unit, key frontend component tests, one smoke e2e suite
answeredQuestions.content-moderation-testing: Azure Content Safety sandbox tier for dev/test

referencesProcessed.keyFindings: 27 business requirements across 7 categories, 5 objectives, 6 KPIs, 7 risks, 3 dependencies, as-is and to-be processes
Design direction: colors (forest green, stone gray, earth brown, campfire orange), typography (Space Grotesk, Source Sans 3), animations, component selection (shadcn/ui), mobile patterns, spacing
conflicts: Old PRD includes offline/PWA and advanced search as in-scope; BRD places these out of scope
Old PRD does not mention dietary restrictions, content moderation, printable recipes, or emailable shopping lists
